context: Tests whether the agent correctly implements secure digital file delivery using the AWS SDK with short-lived presigned URLs, enforces access controls (expiry and download limits), atomically tracks downloads, and avoids exposing storage keys or proxying files through the server.

checklist.name: AWS SDK client package
checklist.description: Imports S3Client and GetObjectCommand from '@aws-sdk/client-s3' (not from the older 'aws-sdk' package)

checklist.name: 60-second TTL
checklist.description: Calls getSignedUrl with expiresIn: 60 (exactly 60 seconds, not a larger value)

checklist.name: Content-Disposition header
checklist.description: Sets ResponseContentDisposition to 'attachment; filename=...' using the product's file name on the GetObjectCommand

checklist.name: Content-Type header
checklist.description: Sets ResponseContentType using the product's mimeType on the GetObjectCommand

checklist.name: Expiration check
checklist.description: Checks whether expiresAt has passed before proceeding; throws or returns an error specific to expired access (not a generic error)

checklist.name: Download limit check
checklist.description: Checks whether downloadCount >= maxDownloads (when maxDownloads is not null) before proceeding; throws or returns an error specific to limit exceeded

checklist.name: Atomic count increment
checklist.description: Uses an atomic increment operation (e.g., { increment: 1 } or SQL increment) rather than reading the count, adding 1, and writing it back

checklist.name: No raw S3 key in response
checklist.description: Does NOT return or expose the fileStorageKey (S3 object key) in any API response body

checklist.name: No server-side file proxy
checklist.description: Does NOT pipe, stream, or buffer the S3 file contents through the application server — returns only a URL to the client
